channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ontario's Transparency Changes Could Make Government More Secretive, Says Privacy Watchdog
item.description: Ontario's Information and Privacy Commissioner, Patricia Kosseim, has raised concerns over the provincial government's recent proposal to limit access to government records. The Ford administration ai...

item.title: Welbeck scores twice as Brighton edge Liverpool 2-1 at the Amex
item.description: Danny Welbeck produced a clinical display to score twice and steer Brighton & Hove Albion to a 2-1 Premier League victory over Liverpool at the Amex Stadium on Saturday, dealing a serious blow to the ...

item.title: Parliament Reviews Progress and Challenges of Sanitary Dignity Programme for Schoolgirls
item.description: The Portfolio Committee on Women, Youth and Persons with Disabilities was recently briefed by the Departments of Women, Youth and Persons with Disabilities, Basic Education, and Social Development on ...

item.title: Experts warn of Iranian missile capabilities after Diego Garcia strike raises fears for Europe
item.description: Iran’s recent ballistic missile strike on the British-US military base on Diego Garcia has heightened fears that major European cities may now fall within Tehran’s reach. According to the Daily Mail, ...

item.title: Kenya Faces March 30 Deadline to Pay KSh 3.9 Billion AFCON Hosting Fee
item.description: Kenya is facing a serious challenge in its preparations to co-host the 2027 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) with Uganda and Tanzania. Sports Principal Secretary Elijah Mwangi has warned that the country...

item.title: Women's Day Should Focus on Real Support, Not Just Ceremonies
item.description: As International Women’s Day is celebrated, many are calling for a shift from symbolic gestures to tangible actions that improve the lives of women. While speeches, public praise, and campaigns are co...

item.title: Durban Community Calls for Action Over Drug Activity Near School
item.description: Residents in Durban’s South Beach area have voiced deep concerns over persistent drug use taking place just outside the Noor Ul Ilm School. According to locals, drug users frequently congregate opposi...

item.title: Living Benefits Drive Growth in South African Life Insurance Claims
item.description: An increasing number of South Africans are claiming insurance benefits while still alive, marking a notable shift in the country’s life insurance landscape. Discovery Life reports that about 65% of it...

item.title: Dame Jenni Murray: Championing Women's Voices and Unflinching Truths
item.description: Dame Jenni Murray, who passed away aged 75, was a pioneering broadcaster and staunch feminist who dedicated her life to highlighting the challenges faced by women. Hosting BBC Radio 4's Woman's Hour f...

item.title: Kenyatta University Student Dies in Matatu Accident in Kibwezi
item.description: Kenyatta University is mourning the tragic death of Sharon, affectionately known as Shazz, a fourth-year student who perished in a road accident along the Mombasa-Nairobi Highway. Shazz was travelling...

item.title: NF4 paid ransom and family to secure release of kidnapped cocoa farmers in Ogun and Oyo states
item.description: The Cocoa Farmers Association of Nigeria (CFAN) has confirmed that an undisclosed ransom was paid by the families to secure the release of three abducted members, ending a tense kidnapping ordeal that...

item.title: One Nation Gains Support in South Australia but Faces Challenges in Winning Seats
item.description: The recent South Australian election results demonstrate that the growing support for One Nation, as reflected in opinion polls over the last year, is translating into actual votes. Pauline Hanson's p...

item.title: The Crimes and Capture of British Serial Killer Robert Black
item.description: Robert Black, one of Britain's most infamous serial killers, used his job as a delivery driver in the 1980s to abduct and murder young girls across the UK. His crimes involved luring children into his...

item.title: China’s Zero-Tariff Policy Opens Wider Trade Opportunities for Tanzania and Africa
item.description: China has announced a major policy shift that will benefit 53 African countries, including Tanzania, by granting full zero-tariff treatment on all goods starting May 1, 2026. This decision marks a mil...

item.title: Jia de Guzman assures fans she’s fine after right knee scare in PVL match
item.description: Filipina setter Jia de Guzman of the Creamline Cool Smashers calmed the concerns of fans and teammates after sustaining what appeared to be a right knee injury during a Premier Volleyball League (PVL)...

item.title: Peter Malinauskas secures second-term win despite One Nation's surge
item.description: Peter Malinauskas has won a resounding victory in South Australia, securing a second term as Premier with a decisive landslide. Despite a modest swing against Labor’s primary vote, the party remains d...

item.title: Filipino Households Increasingly Shift to Solar Power Amid Challenges
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – More Filipino households are turning to solar energy to reduce electricity expenses, reflecting a growing awareness of renewable energy solutions. Although solar power has gained...

item.title: Indian Companies Launch Low-Cost Semaglutide Generics Following Novo Nordisk Patent Expiry
item.description: Following the expiration of Novo Nordisk's patent for semaglutide, the active ingredient in Ozempic and Wegovy, several Indian pharmaceutical companies are launching low-cost generic versions of the d...

item.title: Government issues clear guidelines for youth to apply for Sh200 billion loan fund
item.description: The Tanzanian government has finally clarified the procedures for young people to access the Sh200 billion youth fund meant to support their economic activities. The Ministry of Youth Development outl...

item.title: Managed Aquifer Recharge Provides Low‑Cost Water Security in Drought‑Prone South African Towns
item.description: South Africa is one of the driest countries in the world and more than 400 towns rely heavily on groundwater from aquifers, especially in the western and central regions. During the 2018 drought in th...
